area_anchor: Brikette — Email thread details are stored in a single text blob instead of proper database columns
current_truth: Thread metadata (guest name, booking reference, room numbers, admission outcome, draft stats) is stored as a JSON string in a single metadata_json text column. Every access requires JSON.parse, and none of these fields can be efficiently queried or indexed.
next_scope_now: Investigate which metadata fields are queried most frequently, assess the migration path for moving them to proper columns, and determine backward compatibility requirements.
adjacent_later: Index frequently-filtered metadata columns for faster list queries
Remove metadata_json column once all fields are migrated
evidence_refs: operator-stated: frequently-queried metadata stored as JSON blob, parsed on every access
why: Important details about each email thread — like the guest's name, their booking reference, and room number — are all crammed into one text field. Every time the system needs any of these details, it has to unpack the entire blob. This is slow, error-prone, and makes it impossible to search or filter threads by guest name or booking reference directly. Moving the most-used fields into proper database columns would make the inbox faster and more reliable.

intended_outcome.statement: A validated next action exists for metadata normalization, with target fields identified, migration strategy defined, and backward compatibility approach documented.
